```mermaid
mindmap
  root(Control and Coordination)
    (Importance)
      (Movement as Response to Environment)
        (Examples: Cat running, children playing, buffalo chewing cud)
        (Movement to Advantage: Plants growing into sunshine, children swinging, buffaloes digesting food)
        (Protective Movement: Touching hot object, bright light on eyes)
      (Controlled Movement)
        (Appropriate response to stimuli)
        (Depends on event triggering it)
    (Systems for Control & Coordination)
      (Specialised tissues in multicellular organisms)
      (Animals: Nervous & Muscular Tissues)
      (Plants: Hormonal Control)
    (Nervous System - Animals)
      (Components)
        (Neurons (Nerve Cells))
          (Structure)
            (Dendrites - Acquire Information)
            (Cell Body)
            (Axon - Transmit Electrical Impulse)
            (Axon Terminal - Chemical Signal Release)
          (Function)
            (Conduct information via electrical impulses)
        (Sense Organs)
          (Receptors - Detect environmental changes)
            (Gustatory Receptors - Taste)
            (Olfactory Receptors - Smell)
            (Photoreceptors - Light)
            (Thermoreceptors - Heat)
            (Others)
        (Synapse)
          (Gap between neurons)
          (Transmission of impulse via chemical signals)
      (Process of Nerve Impulse Transmission)
        (Stimulus detected by receptor)
        (Chemical reaction creates electrical impulse)
        (Impulse travels along neuron (dendrite -> cell body -> axon))
        (Electrical impulse at axon terminal triggers release of chemicals)
        (Chemicals cross synapse)
        (Start electrical impulse in next neuron/cell)
      (Types of Actions)
        (Reflex Actions)
          (Sudden, involuntary response)
          (Example: Jumping from hot object)
          (Pathway: Reflex Arc)
            (Receptor -> Sensory Neuron -> Relay Neuron (in spinal cord) -> Motor Neuron -> Effector (Muscle/Gland))
        (Voluntary Actions)
          (Controlled by brain)
          (Example: Writing, speaking)
    (Chemical Coordination - Plants & Animals)
      (Plants)
        (Plant Hormones (Phytohormones))
          (Auxins)
            (Growth at stem tips, phototropism)
          (Gibberellins)
            (Stem elongation, germination)
          (Cytokinins)
            (Cell division, shoot growth)
          (Abscisic Acid)
            (Inhibits growth, stomatal closure, dormancy)
          (Ethylene)
            (Fruit ripening, senescence)
        (Tropisms)
          (Directional growth in response to stimuli)
            (Phototropism - Light)
            (Geotropism - Gravity)
            (Hydrotropism - Water)
            (Thigmotropism - Touch)
      (Animals)
        (Endocrine System)
          (Hormones)
            (Chemical messengers)
            (Produced by endocrine glands)
            (Travel via bloodstream)
            (Act on target cells/organs)
          (Endocrine Glands)
            (Pituitary Gland)
            (Thyroid Gland)
            (Adrenal Glands)
            (Pancreas)
            (Gonads (Testes/Ovaries))
            (Others)
          (Examples of Hormonal Action)
            (Insulin - Regulates blood sugar)
            (Adrenaline - Fight or flight response)
            (Thyroxine - Metabolism)
            (Growth Hormone - Growth)
    (Coordination between Nervous and Hormonal Systems)
      (Nervous system: Fast, short-lived responses)
      (Endocrine system: Slow, long-lasting responses)
      (Integration for overall body function)
```
